3A_Bibliographic.citation: Wojciechowski, T. W. (2024). Traumatic stress exposure as a predictor of dual systems model development: Examining deviant peer association as a social mediating mechanism. Crime & Delinquency. Advance online publication.

3B_Sample.N: 11,877 (full ABCD sample; analytic N varies due to missing data across waves)
3B_Sample.timepoints_used: Wave 1 (baseline, age 9-10), Wave 3 (traumatic stress), Wave 4 (deviant peer association), Wave 5 (sensation-seeking and impulse control outcomes)

3C_Exposure.exposure_measure: NOT a screen time/social media study. Exposure is traumatic stress exposure measured via the K-SADS PTSD subscale at Wave 3 (binary: any traumatic stress yes/no). The keyword match was a false positive - 'social mediating mechanism' in the title was misidentified as 'social media'.
3C_Exposure.how_operationalized: Binary variable from 15 yes/no items on the Kiddie Schedule for Affective Disorders and Schizophrenia PTSD subscale. Coded 1 if any traumatic stressor endorsed.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Dual systems model constructs: sensation-seeking and impulse control at Wave 5
3D_Outcome.measure_used: UPPS Impulsive Behavior Scale (sensation-seeking and lack of premeditation subscales). Index scores from ordinal items.
3D_Outcome.base_rate_if_binary: N/A - continuous outcomes. Traumatic stress prevalence: 33.70% endorsed any traumatic stress at Wave 3.

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Linear SEM with bootstrap resampling (500 resamples) for mediation testing via Preacher and Hayes method. Full-information maximum likelihood for missing data. Deviant peer association at Wave 4 tested as mediator between traumatic stress (Wave 3) and dual systems outcomes (Wave 5).
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: gender, race (Black, Other Race, Latinx ethnicity), parental annual income at baseline, age at Wave 5, prosocial peer association at Wave 4, baseline levels of sensation-seeking and impulse control (Wave 1), deviant peer association at Wave 3
3E_Design_Analysis.SES_in_model: yes - parental annual income at baseline was a covariate
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: The present study utilized structural equation modeling (SEM) to assess direct and indirect relationships of interest. SEM was chosen because of its capacity to delineate the various segments of mediating pathways to compute the individual effects of each component of said pathways and also to be extended to determine the statistical significance of the full mediating pathways.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Traumatic stress (Wave 3) -> impulse control (Wave 5), Model 1 (without mediator). Scale 4-16.
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: experiencing traumatic stress at Wave 3 was associated with diminished impulse control at Wave 5 (coefficient=.142, p<.028)
3F_Effect_Sizes.below_threshold: yes (coefficient 0.141 on a 4-16 scale is very small)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Traumatic stress -> impulse control, Model 2 (with mediator). Attenuated ~25% and no longer significant.
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: traumatic stress exposure on impulse control observed in Model 1 and was no longer significantly associated with lower impulse control (coefficient=.105, p<.097)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Indirect effect: traumatic stress -> deviant peer association -> impulse control
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Deviant peer association did significantly mediate the relationship between traumatic stress exposure and impulse control though (coefficient=.048; Standard error=0.017; p<.006; 95% confidence interval [0.014, 0.082]).
traumatic stress exposure was not a significant predictor of sensation-seeking
Greater deviant peer association at Wave 4 significantly predicted greater sensation-seeking at Wave 5 in this model (coefficient=.339, p<.001)
3F_Effect_Sizes.below_threshold: no (but this is for the mediator, not screen time)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Deviant peer association (Wave 4) -> impulse control (Wave 5), Model 2
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Greater deviant peer association at Wave 4 was also associated with lower impulse control (coefficient=.589, p<.001)
3F_Effect_Sizes.below_threshold: no (but this is for the mediator, not screen time)

3G_Interpretation.verbatim_recommendation: These findings indicate that treatment should be prioritized for youth with histories of traumatic stress exposure. Providing additional funding for public health and criminal justice interventions which screen youth for such histories can facilitate a more effective targeting of treatment programing.

3H_Rhetorical.pattern_detail: This paper is not about screen time or social media. The keyword match was a false positive.

3I_Flags.exclusion_flag: EXCLUDE - This paper does not study screen time or social media as an exposure or outcome. The keyword match was a false positive: 'Social Mediating Mechanism' in the title was detected as 'social media'. The study examines traumatic stress -> deviant peer association -> impulse control/sensation-seeking. Screen time/social media/digital media are not measured or discussed.
3I_Flags.ambiguity_notes: Title contains 'Social Mediating Mechanism' which triggered false positive for 'social media' keyword.
